medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient is a male with a history of prior medical issues, transferred from an outside hospital after a 10-foot fall from scaffolding. He was found to have an open left lower extremity tibia/fibula fracture. He received one dose of Ancef at the outside hospital.

physical_examination.general_examination: On admission, the patient is in pain. Vital signs are stable. Right lower extremity: Skin intact, soft, non-tender thigh and leg, full painless active range of motion/passive range of motion of hip, knee, and ankle. Pulses are intact with a warm and well-perfused foot. Left lower extremity: Open area of skin to medial malleolus, soft, non-tender thigh and leg, full painless active range of motion/passive range of motion of hip, knee, and ankle, intact pulses with a warm and well-perfused foot. On discharge: No acute distress, comfortable. Similar findings were noted in both lower extremities.
